_generated_by: quarterly circularity routine (seeded 2026-06-25 from the ai-capex-groundtruth research pass)
_methodology: gross cash-flow-statement capex (FLOWS not commitment headlines) + off-balance-sheet/debt-financed GPUs - circular double-count (end-node rule + Nvidia<->OpenAI vendor-financing recycling) -> split internal-cash vs NEW-EXTERNAL capital. The dashboard's crowding-out leg = NET EXTERNAL CASH DRAIN (new credit/equity), because internally-funded capex recycles corporate cash and does not compete for the marginal liquidity that bids financial assets.
confidence: medium on the band / low on the point — circularity netting is modeled; always read as a range, never a point
debt_anchor: BIS Bulletin 120 (Jan 2026): 2025 ~ $120B hyperscaler bonds (5x 2024) + ~$120B off-balance-sheet SPV/private credit = ~$240B new external debt; 2026 scales higher as Big-4 FCF goes flat-to-negative and capex approaches ~100% of operating cash flow.
sources: SEC EDGAR XBRL companyfacts/frames (keyless) - data.sec.gov/api/xbrl - capex/OCF/debt per CIK
Epoch AI Hyperscaler Capex vs Cash Flow (CC-BY) - epoch.ai/data-insights/hyperscaler-capex-vs-cash-flow
BIS Bulletin No. 120 'Financing the AI boom' - bis.org/publ/bisbull120.pdf
flags: AI-specific attribution (~75% of public capex) is a SECONDARY industry estimate; companies do not break out AI vs non-AI capex.
Private labs (OpenAI/Anthropic/xAI) file nothing with the SEC - figures attributed to them are press/secondary, widest error band.
Vendor-financing round-trip size (~$30-50B/yr) is the weakest link - Nvidia does not disclose GPU revenue from its own investees.
Two defensible scopes: ~$300-480B new-external-capital (the panel leg) vs ~$450-700B all-AI-resource absorption.
